title: Classes Schema (2024 Rules) - Comprehensive
description: Complete schema capturing ALL information from class markdown files including class descriptions, features, subclasses, spell lists, and all detailed content.

class_description.description: The full narrative description of the class from the markdown. Captures the flavor text explaining what the class represents, their philosophy, role in the world, and what makes them unique. For example, Barbarians are 'mighty warriors who are powered by primal forces of the multiverse that manifest as a Rage. More than a mere emotion—and not limited to anger, this Rage is an incarnation of a predator's ferocity, a storm's fury, and a sea's turmoil.' Bards are experts at 'inspiring others, soothing wounds, disappointing foes, and creating illusions' who believe 'the multiverse was spoken into existence and that remnants of its Words of Creation still resonate and glimmer on every plane of existence.'

core_traits.description:
Core class traits table from the markdown, including:
- primary_ability: The main ability score(s) for the class (e.g., 'Strength' for Barbarian, 'Charisma' for Bard, 'Wisdom' for Cleric, 'Intelligence' for Wizard)
- hit_point_die: The hit die type (D6, D8, D10, D12)
- saving_throw_proficiencies: The saving throw proficiencies (e.g., 'Strength and Constitution' for Barbarian, 'Dexterity and Charisma' for Bard)
- skill_proficiencies: Skill choices available at level 1 (e.g., Barbarian chooses 2 from: Animal Handling, Athletics, Intimidation, Nature, Perception, or Survival)
- weapon_proficiencies: Weapon proficiencies granted (e.g., 'Simple and Martial weapons' for Barbarian)
- armor_training: Armor proficiencies (e.g., 'Light and Medium armor and Shields' for Barbarian)
- tool_proficiencies: Tool proficiencies if any (e.g., 'Herbalism Kit' for Druid)
- starting_equipment: Starting equipment choices with all options detailed (e.g., Barbarian Choice A: Greataxe, 4 Handaxes, Explorer's Pack, and 15 GP; or Choice B: 75 GP)

skill_proficiencies.description: Full text of skill choices, e.g., 'Choose 2: Animal Handling, Athletics, Intimidation, Nature, Perception, or Survival'

hit_die.description: Number value of hit die (6, 8, 10, or 12)

hit_die_type.description: Type of hit die (d6, d8, d10, d12)

proficiency_choices.description: Array of proficiency choices available to the class

proficiencies.description: List of proficiencies granted by the class

class_levels.description:
Complete level progression from 1-20 with ALL features at each level. Each level entry contains:
- level: The character level (1-20)
- proficiency_bonus: The proficiency bonus at this level (+2, +3, +4, +5, +6)
- features: Array of feature objects, each containing:
  - name: The feature name (e.g., 'Rage', 'Unarmored Defense', 'Fury', 'Bear')
  - description: The FULL, unaltered description of the feature from the markdown
  - level: The level at which this feature is gained
  - type: The type of feature (e.g., 'class_feature', 'subclass_feature', 'martial_choice')

Example Barbarian Level 1 features:
- RAGE: 'You can imbue yourself with a primal power called Rage, a force that grants you extraordinary might and resilience. You can enter it as a Bonus Action if you aren't wearing Heavy armor. You can enter your Rage the number of times shown for your Barbarian level in the Rages column. You Regain one expended use when you finish a Short Rest, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. While active, your Rage follows the rules below: Damage Resistance - You have Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. Rage Damage - When you make an attack using Strength and deal damage, you gain a bonus to the damage that increases as you gain levels. Strength Advantage - You have Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws. No Concentration or Spells - You can't maintain Concentration, and you can't cast spells. Duration - The Rage lasts until the end of your next turn, and it ends early if you don Heavy armor or have the Incapacitated condition.'
- UNARMED DEFENSE: 'While you aren't wearing any armor, your base Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Constitution modifiers.'
- WEAPON MASTERY: 'Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial Melee weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.'

majors.description:
Array of major choices (replaces traditional subclasses in 2024 rules). Each major includes:
- name: The major/subclass name (e.g., 'Path of the Berserker', 'College of Lore')
- subtitle: The subtitle/tagline (e.g., 'Channel Rage into Violent Fury', 'Plumb the Depths of Magical Knowledge')
- description: The full description of what this subclass represents and its philosophy
- features: Array of all subclass features with full descriptions
- spells: If the subclass grants prepared spells, the complete list organized by level

minor_choices.description: Array of minor choices (replaces traditional subclasses in 2024 rules)

spellcasting.description:
Complete spellcasting information for the class. Contains:
- spellcasting_ability: The ability used for spellcasting (e.g., 'Charisma' for Bard, 'Wisdom' for Cleric, 'Intelligence' for Wizard)
- spellcasting_type: Whether full, half, or third caster
- spell_slots: Complete spell slot progression table showing slots per spell level for all levels
- cantrips_known: Number of cantrips known at each level
- spells_known: Number of prepared/spells known at each level
- spell_list: The COMPLETE spell list from the markdown, organized by spell level. Each spell entry contains:
  - name: Spell name (e.g., 'Guidance', 'Sacred Flame', 'Fireball')
  - school: School of magic (e.g., 'Divination', 'Evocation', 'Conjuration')
  - special: Special tags (e.g., 'C' for Concentration, 'R' for Ritual, 'M' for Material component)
- ritual_casting: Whether the class can cast rituals
- spellcasting_focus: What can be used as a spellcasting focus (e.g., 'Holy Symbol', 'Musical Instrument', 'Arcane Focus', 'Druidic Focus')
- spell_preparation_rules: Rules for preparing/changing spells (e.g., Cleric: 'Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change your list of prepared spells')

spell_slots.description: Full spell slot table for all levels

spell_list.description: Complete spell list organized by level. Each level contains array of spell objects with name, school, and special tags

hit_points_at_higher_levels.description: Hit point calculation for higher levels (2024 rules)

proficiency_bonus_scaling.description: How proficiency bonus scales with level (2024 rules)

martial_choices.description:
Array of martial choices (combat styles, fighting styles). Each entry contains:
- name: The feat/option name (e.g., 'Defense', 'Blade Ward')
- description: Full description of the option
- prerequisite: Any prerequisites required

equipment_options.description: Equipment choices available at character creation

feature_levels.description: Class features gained at each level with full descriptions

multiclassing.description:
Multiclassing requirements and rules. Contains:
- requirements: Prerequisites for multiclassing into this class
- traits_gained: Which core traits are gained when multiclassing
- features_gained: Which level 1 features are gained when multiclassing

core_traits_gained.description: Which core traits multiclass characters gain

features_gained.description: Which level 1 features multiclass characters gain

level_1_features.description:
Features gained at 1st level with FULL descriptions. Each feature contains:
- name: Feature name
- description: Complete unaltered description from markdown
- details: Any sub-options, tables, or additional mechanics

level_2_features.description: Features gained at 2nd level with FULL descriptions

level_3_features.description: Features gained at 3rd level with FULL descriptions, including subclass features

level_4_features.description: Features gained at 4th level with FULL descriptions

level_5_features.description: Features gained at 5th level with FULL descriptions

level_6_features.description: Features gained at 6th level with FULL descriptions

level_7_features.description: Features gained at 7th level with FULL descriptions

level_8_features.description: Features gained at 8th level with FULL descriptions

level_9_features.description: Features gained at 9th level with FULL descriptions

level_10_features.description: Features gained at 10th level with FULL descriptions

level_14_features.description: Features gained at 14th level with FULL descriptions

level_18_features.description: Features gained at 18th level with FULL descriptions

level_19_features.description: Features gained at 19th level with FULL descriptions (Epic Boon)

level_20_features.description: Features gained at 20th level (capstone) with FULL descriptions

subclass_features.description:
ALL subclass features with complete descriptions. For each subclass:
- subclass_name: Name of the subclass (e.g., 'Path of the Berserker', 'College of Lore')
- subtitle: The thematic subtitle (e.g., 'Channel Rage into Violent Fury')
- description: Full narrative description of the subclass philosophy and role
- features: Array of all features with:
  - name: Feature name with level (e.g., 'LEVEL 3: FRENZY')
  - description: COMPLETE unaltered description from markdown
  - level: Level gained
  - options: If the feature has choices, all options with full descriptions
- spells: If subclass grants subclass spells, the complete table with level and spell names
- additional_content: Any additional tables, options, or mechanics (e.g., Wild Magic Surge table, maneuver options, invocation options)

maneuver_options.description:
For classes with maneuver systems (e.g., Battle Master Fighter), all maneuver options with:
- name: Maneuver name (e.g., 'Ambush', 'Bait and Switch', 'Commander's Strike')
- description: Complete description of what the maneuver does, including dice costs, saving throws, and effects

invocation_options.description:
For Warlock, all Eldritch Invocation options with:
- name: Invocation name (e.g., 'Agonizing Blast', 'Devil's Sight')
- prerequisite: Prerequisites required
- description: Complete description of the invocation's effects

metamagic_options.description:
For Sorcerer, all Metamagic options with:
- name: Metamagic name (e.g., 'Careful Spell', 'Empowered Spell')
- cost: Sorcery Point cost
- description: Complete description of what the metamagic does

wild_magic_surge_table.description: For Wild Magic Sorcerer, the complete Wild Magic Surge table with ranges and effects

beast_shapes.description: For Druid, the Beast Shapes table showing known forms and max CR by level

star_map_options.description: For Circle of the Stars Druid, Star Map form options and Starry Form constellation options

nature_ward_resistances.description: For Circle of the Land Druid, Nature's Ward resistance table by land type

feywild_gifts.description: For Fey Wanderer Ranger, the Feywild Gifts table
